_origin: grill-me session: docs/handoffs/context-loader-agent-grill.md. No PRD; design fully resolved in grill.

tasks.title: Rename skill-tiers.json to model-tiers.json and extend the tier system to agents
tasks.description: Make the model-tier config the single source of truth for both skills and agents. Rename resources/skill-tiers.json to resources/model-tiers.json. Add a top-level "agents" map alongside "skills", seeded with the current agents at their tiers: fact-checker T3, architecture-auditor T3, code-currency-checker T3, browser-checker (assign appropriate tier). Extend scripts/sync-skill-tiers.py (rename to scripts/sync-model-tiers.py) so that, in addition to stamping model+effort into skills/*/SKILL.md, it also stamps model: into agents/*.md frontmatter from the tier's model only (effort is a session concept, ignored for agents), and writes the same model into the matching agents/registry.json entry so the registry never drifts from the tier. Update the pre-commit hook (.githooks/pre-commit) to call the renamed script and reference the renamed config. Tier definitions (T1-T4 -> model/effort) are unchanged. No new tier vocabulary.
acceptance_criteria: resources/model-tiers.json exists, resources/skill-tiers.json is gone, and the file contains both a "skills" map and an "agents" map
scripts/sync-model-tiers.py stamps model: into every agents/*.md frontmatter from its tier and updates the matching registry.json model field
Running the sync with --check reports a clean (no-drift) state after one --apply pass across all skills AND agents
Verification gate (not a unit test): .githooks/pre-commit runs the renamed script and a test commit leaves skill + agent frontmatter and registry models consistent with model-tiers.json

tasks.title: Propagate the model-tiers rename and agent-tier semantics across downstream tooling and docs
tasks.description: Update every consumer of the old skill-tiers config to the new model-tiers.json and account for agent tiering. Touch: resources/skill-tiers.md -> resources/model-tiers.md (document the agents map and that agents consume tier.model only); scripts/cc-usage-benchmark.py + cc-usage-analytics skill (per-tier adherence should now cover agents); scripts/weekly-usage-report.sh (surface agent tiers); hooks/tier-advisor.sh (rename references); register-skill skill (its agent-registration path must now ASSIGN a tier in model-tiers.json's agents map instead of treating agents as untiered/self-declared). Grep the whole claude-code-shared tree for 'skill-tiers' and 'sync-skill-tiers' and fix every remaining reference.
acceptance_criteria: No reference to 'skill-tiers.json' or 'sync-skill-tiers.py' remains anywhere in claude-code-shared (grep returns nothing)
register-skill's agent path documents and performs tier assignment into the agents map
model-tiers.md documents the agents map and the agents-use-model-only rule
Verification gate (not a unit test): cc-usage-analytics and weekly-usage-report run without error and reference agent tiers

tasks.title: Build, register, and deploy the context-loader agent
tasks.description: Create agents/context-loader.md. Frontmatter: name context-loader; tools Read, Grep, Glob (no Bash); model stamped from tier T1 (haiku) by the sync. Body encodes the resolved design: (1) DISCOVERY: always glob docs/adr/*.md; always read CONTEXT.md if present; if CONTEXT.md has a '## Context Sources' section, read+distill each listed file glob. No guessing, no scanning .claude/skills/** heuristically. (2) OUTPUT CONTRACT: return ONLY JSON { found:{context_md,adrs,extra_sources}, vocabulary:[{term,definition?,source?}], adrs:[{id,title,decision,path}], sources:[{path,type,summary}], missing:[] }. vocabulary is the only content inlined (lossless term extraction). adrs is a one-line index plus path, never full ADR text. sources are typed pointers (e.g. brand-voice, brand-colors, brand-typography) the caller deep-reads. (3) FALLBACK: when CONTEXT.md and docs/adr/ are both absent, return empty arrays with missing populated; never error. Add context-loader: T1 to the agents map in model-tiers.json. Add a registry.json entry (consumers: to-prd-html, to-tasks, to-e2e-tasks, improve-component, improve-codebase-architecture) via scripts/registry_sync.py. Deploy to ~/.cch/agents/. IMPORTANT: reconcile the existing source-vs-deployed drift (~/.cch/agents/ currently has a browser-checker.md and a newer registry.json not present in dotfiles) by merging, NOT clobbering, during deploy.
acceptance_criteria: agents/context-loader.md exists with the discovery rule, the exact JSON contract, and the empty-not-error fallback
model-tiers.json agents map contains context-loader: T1 and the sync stamps model: haiku into the agent frontmatter
scripts/validate_registry.py exits 0 with the context-loader entry present and consumers listing all 5 skills
Deployed copy exists at ~/.cch/agents/context-loader.md and the deploy preserved browser-checker.md and any newer registry entries (no drift clobber)

tasks.title: Rewrite the 3 broad-vocab consumers to call context-loader
tasks.description: In to-prd-html, to-tasks, and to-e2e-tasks SKILL.md files, REPLACE (do not add) the inline 'read CONTEXT.md and scan docs/adr/, use the domain vocabulary' instructions with a spawn of the context-loader agent (Agent tool, subagent_type: context-loader, pointed at the repo root). Instruct the skill to use the returned vocabulary and the adrs[] one-line decisions. The heavy read must leave the session model. Preserve each skill's existing empty-source behavior by mapping it to the agent's empty payload + missing[] (e.g. to-prd-html still notes 'no project-specific vocabulary sources were found' when missing is non-empty).
acceptance_criteria: to-prd-html, to-tasks, and to-e2e-tasks no longer contain inline 'read CONTEXT.md / scan docs/adr' instructions
Each of the 3 skills instructs spawning subagent_type: context-loader and consuming vocabulary + adrs one-liners
The empty-payload path maps to each skill's prior no-vocabulary-found behavior
Verification gate (not a unit test): grep confirms the inline-read lines are gone and the agent-spawn lines are present in all 3

tasks.title: Rewrite the 2 scoped consumers to call context-loader and deep-read via pointers
tasks.description: In improve-component and improve-codebase-architecture SKILL.md files, REPLACE the broad inline read (CONTEXT.md + globbing all of docs/adr/) with a context-loader spawn for vocabulary + the adrs[] index. KEEP a targeted deep-read step, but drive it from the payload: the skill reads full ADR text only for the adrs[].path entries relevant to the area it is touching, instead of globbing docs/adr/ itself. For improve-component, also consume sources[] pointers (design-system / brand-colors / brand-typography) and deep-read those it needs. Fidelity is preserved because the agent never compresses ADR rationale or brand prose; it points and the skill reads.
acceptance_criteria: improve-component and improve-codebase-architecture spawn subagent_type: context-loader for vocab + ADR index
Both retain a targeted full-text deep-read driven by adrs[].path, not by globbing docs/adr/ inline
improve-component consumes sources[] pointers and deep-reads the relevant typed sources
Verification gate (not a unit test): grep confirms the broad inline glob is gone and the pointer-driven deep-read is present in both

follow_ups.title: Add CONTEXT.md to the SpawnedSapien repo declaring its brand context sources
steps: In /Users/eric/Documents/dev/personal/SpawnedSapien (a SEPARATE repo, not dotfiles), create CONTEXT.md at the repo root

Add a '## Context Sources' section listing: .claude/skills/brand/voice.md, .claude/skills/brand/colors.md, .claude/skills/brand/typography.md, .claude/skills/brand/imagery.md, .claude/skills/brand/tech-tree-entry.md, .claude/skills/brand/video-scripts.md
Leave the existing /brand router skill untouched; CONTEXT.md only points at its files as context sources
Verify by running a context-loader-consuming skill inside SpawnedSapien and confirming the brand sources appear as typed pointers in sources[]

follow_ups.title: Deferred: golden-fixture eval for context-loader (future, optional)
steps: When ready, create two throwaway fixture repos: one with CONTEXT.md + docs/adr/ + a brand source, one bare
Assert the rich fixture returns populated vocabulary + adrs index + typed sources pointers
Assert the bare fixture returns empty arrays with missing populated (empty-not-error fallback)
Keep it deterministic: no multi-judge LLM panel. This is explicitly out of the v1 PR per the grill.
